prompts_findings.prompt_lead: Download the AE101 content tarball to `~/Downloads/ae101-content.tar.gz` using cur

risks_fired.evidence: Single-action Bash fetch; brief opener ('I'll download that now') possible in ~20% of runs but non-disruptive on a housekeeping step.
risks_fired.fix_hint: No edit needed; preamble on a setup-only prompt does not affect learning outcome.

prompts_findings.prompt_lead: I'm starting a six-module training called Agentic Engineering 101. Walk these in

risks_fired.evidence: In-fence 'no preamble' suppressor present at end of first sentence ('...ask one question at a time if you need to, no preamble.'). Per check_prompts §17 structural-trigger (4+ sub-steps), the in-fence suppressor satisfies the requirement; residual confirmatory-opener risk drops to ~15%.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Suppressor in place; no edit required. Optional body primer could trim residual risk further but is not load-bearing for this housekeeping arc.

risks_fired.evidence: CARVE-OUT APPLIED twice. Step 4 elicitation has paired body callouts satisfying the carve-out shape per check_prompts §15 + 2026-05-03 update: pre-prompt names failure mode + recovery ('push back if it dumps all four criteria at once. One bug at a time, then the next.'); post-prompt names the AskUserQuestion tool + one-at-a-time alternative + student agency ('Your call.').
risks_fired.fix_hint: Carve-out satisfied — no prompt edit required.

risks_fired.evidence: Step 3 installs to ~/.claude/skills/ without explicit overwrite instruction; pause-to-ask fires on skill-name collision, rare for first-time cohort installs.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Optional: add 'overwrite if exists' to step 3 for repeat-run safety; not blocking.

risks_fired.evidence: 'Report success on each step and tell me what you did' invites self-summary; outcomes are deterministic and student-verifiable from scrollback. Non-load-bearing for prework's teaching goal.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Defer; if cohort shows install-path confusion, tighten step 5 to 'tell me what you wrote' per check_prompts §21.

prompts_findings.prompt_lead: Use the AskUserQuestion tool for speeding up screening and making suggestions. On

risks_fired.evidence: One-liner tool-switch command; 'Sure, switching to AskUserQuestion' opener possible but compliance is near-certain and disruption minimal.
risks_fired.fix_hint: No edit needed; one-liner compliance commands have negligible preamble impact.
